title: Sports Bodies and NEP 2020
focus: Details the structure of national sports bodies and the targets and initiatives under the National Education Policy 2020.
rationale: Fallback section derived from packet-level grouping.
sectionExcerpt:
[Page 052]
National Sports Election Panel to oversee elections of national sports bodies must consist of at least 20 members at all times.
Disqualification criteria: Persons convicted by a court followed by a sentence of imprisonment.
Structure of Every National Sports Bodies
- General Body: Consists equal number of representatives from each of affiliate units and ex officio members.
- Ethics Committee
- Athletes Committee
- Secretary General: Head of administration responsible for carrying out all decisions of Executive Committee and General Body.
- Executive Committee: Atleast fifteen members, (atleast 4 women), atleast 2 sportspersons, 2 from athletes committee and ex officio and other members.
- Dispute Resolution Committee
- President: Head of concerned National Sports Body and preside over meetings of its Executive Committee and General Body.
- Treasurer: Custodian of funds or accounts of concerned National Sports Body.
For more details on Key Regulatory Bodies in Sports Governance, refer to appendix
7.5. 5 YEARS OF NATIONAL EDUCATION POLICY (NEP)
Why in the News?
National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 completed 5 years.
About NEP 2020
- Drafted on recommendations of Kasturirangan Committee, it is country’s third education policy post-independence (first 2 policies in 1968 and 1986 modified in 1992).
Key Focus Areas of NEP 2020
Sector: School Education
- ECCE: National Curricular and Pedagogical Framework for Early Childhood Care and Education (NCPFECCE) for children up to 8 years.
- Foundational Literacy & Numeracy (FLN): Reading with comprehension and basic math by Grade 3; DIKSHA platform in 133 languages.
- Curricular Structure: 5+3+3+4 system; National Curricular Framework for School Education by NCERT.
- Multilingualism: Mother tongue/local language as medium till Grade 5 (preferably Grade 8+).
- Assessment Reforms: PARAKH as national assessment and standard-setting body.
- Teachers: Pupil-Teacher Ratio ≤ 30:1; minimum 50 hours Continuous Professional Development annually.

[Page 053]
Standards & Accreditation
- State-level Department of School Education for monitoring and policymaking.
Higher Education
- Quality Institutions: 3/4-year UG degrees with multiple exit options; Academic Bank of Credits (ABC).
- Teacher Education: Online training via SWAYAM/DIKSHA; National Mission for Mentoring.
- Regulatory Reform: Higher Education Commission of India (HECI) with four verticals.
- Other Areas: NCIVE for vocational integration; NETF for ed-tech integ...
